result.summary: Zendesk exposes tenant-scoped REST APIs for Ticketing, Help Center, and Chat, a public unauthenticated Status API, Sell REST APIs at api.getbase.com plus a Sell GraphQL endpoint at /v3/graphql, and the ZCLI CLI; auth is via Zendesk API token + email over Basic or Zendesk OAuth bearer for core APIs, Chat OAuth or Zendesk OAuth bearer for Chat, Sell personal access token or Sell OAuth bearer for Sell/GraphQL, and no auth for Status.

support_api_token.label: Zendesk API token
support_api_token.setup: In Zendesk Admin Center, go to **Apps and integrations** → **APIs** → **Zendesk API** and create a token. Zendesk documents API tokens in [Security and authentication](https://developer.zendesk.com/api-reference/introduction/security-and-auth/). When using it against Zendesk REST APIs, send it with your Zendesk account email via HTTP Basic auth as ``{email}/token:{api_token}``.

support_oauth.label: Zendesk OAuth access token
support_oauth.setup: Create an OAuth client in your Zendesk instance and complete a Zendesk OAuth flow as described in [Security and authentication](https://developer.zendesk.com/api-reference/introduction/security-and-auth/) and [creating and using OAuth tokens with the API](https://developer.zendesk.com/documentation/api-basics/authentication/creating-and-using-oauth-tokens-with-the-api/). Use the resulting access token as a bearer token.

sell_pat.label: Sell personal access token
sell_pat.setup: Sign in to Sell, open **Integrations** → **OAuth**, then in **Access Tokens** click **+ Add User Token** to mint a personal access token, as shown in [Make your first call](https://developer.zendesk.com/documentation/sales-crm/first-call/). Copy the token when shown; after closing the modal it cannot be retrieved again. Send it as ``Authorization: Bearer {token}``.

sell_oauth_app.label: Sell OAuth 2.0 access token
sell_oauth_app.setup: Register an application in Sell at [app.futuresimple.com/settings/oauth/apps](https://app.futuresimple.com/settings/oauth/apps) to get a `client_id` and `client_secret`, then complete one of Sell's OAuth 2.0 flows to obtain an access token, as documented in [OAuth 2.0 Introduction](https://developer.zendesk.com/api-reference/sales-crm/authentication/introduction/). Use the access token as ``Authorization: Bearer {access_token}``.

chat_oauth.label: Zendesk Chat OAuth token
chat_oauth.setup: Create a Zendesk Chat OAuth application and complete the Chat OAuth flow described in [OAuth Authentication](https://developer.zendesk.com/documentation/live-chat/getting-started/auth/). Use the resulting access token as ``Authorization: Bearer {access_token}`` for Chat API requests.

variables.description: Use the brand-specific subdomain for multi-brand help centers.

requiredHeaders.name: Accept
requiredHeaders.description: Sell APIs require an Accept header indicating JSON responses.
